incident_description: While driving at a steady 55 mph on Route 9 with no other vehicle, object, or road-surface contact involved, the engine lost power and seized. There was no collision, no impact, and no accident of any kind — the vehicle simply stopped running and was towed. Subsequent teardown attributed the failure to a worn timing chain and chronic oil starvation that developed gradually over time.
original_denial_reason: Claim denied per §7.3 — Mechanical Failure Exclusion. Teardown confirms gradual wear-and-tear (worn timing chain, chronic oil starvation); no covered collision event occurred, so no exception applies.

supporting_docs.summary: Engine failure caused by a worn timing chain and long-term oil starvation with heavy sludge buildup — classic gradual deterioration. NO evidence of collision or external impact damage anywhere on the vehicle. Failure is consistent with deferred maintenance, not a sudden external event.
Oil service overdue by approximately 14,000 miles. Two prior dashboard oil-pressure warnings logged and not acted upon. No record of the timing chain ever being inspected.
